models.code:
class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    Id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    Name = Column(String)
    Balance = Column(Decimal, default=0)
    CreditLimit = Column(Decimal)
models.description: Represents a customer with credit balance management.
models.name: Customer

models.code:
class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    Id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    CustomerId = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.Id'))
    AmountTotal = Column(Decimal, default=0)
    DateShipped = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    Notes = Column(String)
models.description: Represents an order with a link to a customer.
models.name: Order

models.code:
class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'items'

    Id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    OrderId = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.Id'))
    ProductId = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.Id'))
    Amount = Column(Decimal, default=0)
    Quantity = Column(Integer)
    UnitPrice = Column(Decimal)
models.description: Represents an item in an order.
models.name: Item

models.code:
class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    Id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    Name = Column(String)
    UnitPrice = Column(Decimal)
models.description: Represents a product that can be ordered.
models.name: Product

rules.name: CustomerCreditLimitConstraint
rules.description: The Customer's balance must be less than the credit limit.
rules.code:
Rule.constraint(validate=Customer,
    as_condition=lambda row: row.Balance <= row.CreditLimit,
    error_msg='Customer balance ({row.Balance}) exceeds credit limit ({row.CreditLimit})')

rules.name: CustomerBalanceDerivation
rules.description: Derive Customer Balance as the sum of Order Amount where date shipped is null.
rules.code: Rule.sum(derive=Customer.Balance, as_sum_of=Order.AmountTotal, where=lambda row: row.DateShipped is None)

rules.name: OrderAmountTotalDerivation
rules.description: Derive Order Amount Total as the sum of Item Amount.

rules.name: ItemAmountDerivation
rules.description: Derive Item Amount as the product of Quantity and Unit Price.

rules.name: ItemUnitPriceCopy
rules.description: Copy Item's Unit Price from Product's Unit Price.

rules.name: OrderAppIntegrationEvent
rules.description: Send the Order details to Kafka when the DateShipped is not None.
rules.code:
Rule.after_flush_row_event(on_class=Order, calling=kafka_producer.send_row_to_kafka,
    if_condition=lambda row: row.DateShipped is not None,
    with_args={'topic': 'order_shipping'})

test_data:
# Test Data
from datetime import date

# Customers
customer1 = Customer(Id=1, Name="Alice", Balance=100, CreditLimit=500)
customer2 = Customer(Id=2, Name="Bob", Balance=200, CreditLimit=400)
customer3 = Customer(Id=3, Name="Charlie", Balance=0, CreditLimit=300)
customer4 = Customer(Id=4, Name="Diana", Balance=250, CreditLimit=250)

# Products
product1 = Product(Id=1, Name="Widget", UnitPrice=10)
product2 = Product(Id=2, Name="Gadget", UnitPrice=20)
product3 = Product(Id=3, Name="Doodad", UnitPrice=15)
product4 = Product(Id=4, Name="Thingamajig", UnitPrice=5)

# Orders
order1 = Order(Id=1, CustomerId=1, AmountTotal=50, DateShipped=date(2023, 10, 15), Notes="Urgent")
order2 = Order(Id=2, CustomerId=2, AmountTotal=0, DateShipped=None, Notes="Backorder")
order3 = Order(Id=3, CustomerId=3, AmountTotal=150, DateShipped=date(2023, 10, 10), Notes="")
order4 = Order(Id=4, CustomerId=4, AmountTotal=0, DateShipped=None, Notes="Promotional")

# Items
item1 = Item(Id=1, OrderId=1, ProductId=1, Amount=20, Quantity=2, UnitPrice=10)
item2 = Item(Id=2, OrderId=2, ProductId=2, Amount=0, Quantity=0, UnitPrice=20)
item3 = Item(Id=3, OrderId=3, ProductId=3, Amount=90, Quantity=6, UnitPrice=15)
item4 = Item(Id=4, OrderId=4, ProductId=4, Amount=50, Quantity=10, UnitPrice=5)
